title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
summary: A podcast episode discussing whether AI agents should manage passwords and detect compromised credentials, examining cybersecurity hygiene practices. It covers Microsoft's largest Patch Tuesday with 200+ CVEs and explores C-suite executives' willingness to accept cyber risk for innovation gains.
writeup: This Security Intelligence podcast episode from IBM Technology debates whether AI agents should be trusted to detect compromised passwords and change them automatically, weighing the promise of automated identity management against its pitfalls. The panel also examines Microsoft's record-breaking June 2026 Patch Tuesday, which addressed over 200 CVEs, and reviews a report finding that C-suite executives are increasingly willing to accept cyber risk in exchange for innovation gains. The discussion ties together agentic automation in credential handling, large-scale vulnerability remediation, and shifting executive attitudes toward risk.
